telegramText: MARKET WRAP — Apr 22, 2026 Markets reflected growing institutional acceptance of crypto as a legitimate asset class, with Bitcoin's surge above $78K driven by record ETF inflows coinciding with broader risk-on sentiment. Traditional safe havens like gold also rallied alongside equities, suggesting investors are positioning for potential policy shifts under evolving geopolitical dynamics. CLOSING PRICES BTC: $78,482 · ETH: $2,393 · SOL: $87.17 · GOLD: $4,740 · SPY: $711.21 (+0.4%) · QQQ: $655.11 (+1.3%) · VIX: $18.92 (-3.0%) · TLT: $86.74 (-0.4%) · DXY: $98.61 (+0.2%) · OIL: $92.87 (+0.8%) EVENING NEWS ▼ Bitcoin Institutional Race: Latest BTC Buy Pushes Strategy Ahead Of BlackRock ▲ BlackRock Bitcoin ETF Holdings Hit Record 806,700 BTC Worth $63.7 Billion ▲ Trump-Backed American Bitcoin Stock Rockets 14% As Firm Ramps Up BTC Mining ▼ US Trade Czar Threatens Canada Over Boycott That’s Hurting Booze Makers ▲ BlackRock buys $900M in Bitcoin via iShares Bitcoin Trust — WEX Q1 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Rise SCENARIO SHIFTS ▲ Late Cycle: Institutional Bitcoin adoption suggests late-cycle asset diversification and inflation hedging ▲ Goldilocks: Record institutional crypto flows indicate stable economic conditions supporting risk assets ▲ Stagflation: Trade tensions and Bitcoin as inflation hedge both point to stagflationary pressures ▲ Hegemony: US asserting trade dominance while building crypto leadership through institutional adoption ▲ Displacement: Bitcoin institutional acceptance represents major displacement of traditional store-of-value assets REGIME CHECK ▲ Risk-On · Neutral · OVERHEATING · Gauge: 0/100 UPCOMING — HIGH IMPACT US ISM Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index(Apr) — May 1, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: 52.7) Producer Price Index (YoY)(Q1) — May 1, 01:30 AM UTC (prev: 3.5%) US Personal consumption expenditure price index (AQR)(Preliminary)(Q1) — Apr 30, 12:30 PM UTC US Real GDP(AQR)(Preliminary)(Q1) — Apr 30, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 0.5%) US Personal income (MoM)(Mar) — Apr 30, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: -0.1%) US Personal consumption expenditure (MoM)(Mar) — Apr 30, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 0.5%) CONTRARIAN • Massive institutional Bitcoin buying at current levels could signal a local top as smart money distr • Trade tensions with Canada may be theatrical negotiating tactics rather than genuine economic policy agentcanary.ai | Not financial advice
